questions.prompt: What should the goal consider in scope while resolving the open issue queue?
questions.description: The live queue includes implementation tickets, architecture-deepening tickets, documentation tickets, one benchmark follow-up ticket, and at least one ready-for-human decision ticket.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Resolve every open issue that can be completed by an agent, while explicitly triaging or escalating ready-for-human/question issues instead of forcing them into implementation.

questions.prompt: How should implementation work be batched?
questions.description: The current queue has natural dependency clusters: Electron target stack (#6-#20), deterministic e2e/bridge command stack (#21-#29 plus #35), and architecture deepening (#30-#34).
questions.recommendedAnswer: Use small dependency-ordered PR batches: one coherent vertical slice at a time, with validation and issue closure per batch.

questions.prompt: What priority order should the goal plan assume?
questions.description: The issue queue contains overlapping older implementation issues and newer architecture follow-ups.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Start with the deterministic e2e / Web Agent benchmark path because it tightens validation for later work, then architecture deepening that reduces future friction, then Electron stack work in dependency order.

questions.prompt: What counts as done for each issue or batch?
questions.description: The repository has strict local rules for checks, builds, changelog/docs, GitNexus impact/change detection, and commits.
questions.recommendedAnswer: For each batch: implement only the scoped slice, run required impact analysis before symbol edits, run targeted tests during development, run full required checks/builds before commit, update docs/changelog when behavior changes, push, and close issues only after validation passes.

questions.prompt: How should blocked, question, or ready-for-human issues be handled?
questions.description: Some open issues have blocked/question/ready-for-human labels or depend on other issues.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Do not implement blocked/HITL issues until prerequisites or decisions are resolved; instead record the blocker, ask for the smallest needed decision, and continue with the next unblocked issue.
